mission.title: containerd: ExecStartPre=/sbin/modprobe fails inside LXC
mission.description: ExecStartPre=/sbin/modprobe fails inside LXC . This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify containerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
**Description**

Running Debian 9.5 in a LXC container. Upgraded `docker-ce` to `5:18.09.0~3-0~debian-stretch`, which pulls `containerd.io` version `1.2.0-1`.

1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for ExecStartPre=/sbin/modprobe fails inside LXC
steps.description:
The packaging for docker ce/ee would go into ~~https://github.com/docker/containerd-packaging/blob/master/common/containerd.service#L7~~ sorry, just realised that repo isn't public; content looks the same as is in this repository though :smile:

> It looks like adding a `-` to the ExecStartPre command is something we can easily do it our service file 

Oh, TIL (https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=651797#c2)

> If you prepend the path with a dash the result will be ignored:
>

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm ExecStartPre=/sbin/modprobe fails inside LXC  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The packaging for docker ce/ee would go into ~~https://github.com/docker/containerd-packaging/blob/master/common/containerd.service#L7~~ sorry, just realised that repo isn't public; content looks the same as is in this repository though :smile:

> It looks like adding a `-` to the ExecStartPre command is something we can easily do it our service file 

Oh, TIL

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
